name: Lusitanians
startIntroPart1: From the mountains of western Iberia, the Lusitanians defied the most powerful empire the world had ever seen. Masters of guerrilla warfare, they struck without warning and vanished into the hills before Rome could answer. Their leader, Viriato, rose from shepherd to commander, undefeated in open battle, betrayed only by those he trusted.
startIntroPart2: I am Viriato. I have no palace, no senate, no legions of marble. I have the mountains, and I have my people. Rome calls us barbarians — let them. We will show them what barbarians do to empires.
declaringWar: You have made a grave mistake. The mountains remember every Roman who entered them. None returned.
introduction: I am Viriato, voice of the free tribes of Lusitania. We are not a nation built of stone and marble — we are born of the mountain wind. What do you want from us?
attacked: You attack us in our own hills? Then you will die in our hills. Every rock, every ravine, every forest path belongs to us.
defeated: They could not defeat us in battle. They bought our deaths instead. Remember that, when you celebrate your victory.
denounced: You have chosen poorly. The mountains have long memories.
neutralHello: The mountains are quiet today. Let us keep it that way.
hateHello: What do you want from us?
tradeRequest: Perhaps there is something of value to discuss.
uniques: When defeating a [Barbarian] unit, earn [0] Gold and recruit it

civilopediaText.text: The Lusitanians were an Indo-European people of the western Iberian Peninsula, inhabiting what is today central Portugal and parts of western Spain. Fierce and independent, they resisted Roman conquest for decades, mastering guerrilla tactics in the rugged terrain of their homeland. Their greatest leader, Viriato, rose from humble origins to become the most dangerous enemy Rome faced in Iberia — undefeated in battle, he was ultimately betrayed and assassinated in 139 BC at Roman instigation. The Lusitanians left no written records of their own, but their spirit endures in the land and people of Portugal.
